content: Autonomous curiosity-driven ICM agents can dynamically prioritize which episodic memories to compress and retain long-term by framing compression effort as a measure of prediction error, turning memory summarization itself into a intrinsic reward signal. This means an agent only retains and compresses memories that still contain unresolved surprise, rather than wasting limited long-term memory storage on predictable, low-information experiences.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
